summary_strategy: Position Conner as a pragmatic DevSecOps engineer who bridges security and platform engineering by shipping production-grade automation on GitLab-like platforms, emphasizing cross-functional delivery and measurable risk reduction
skills_strategy: Use condensed 3-line format to group skills around three high-impact themes: automation & delivery, platform security, and data-driven SecOps; emphasize Python/PowerShell, CI/CD, GitLab-native patterns (e.g., pipelines, merge request workflows), and platform hardening without listing every tool

experience_focus.must_highlight: production deployment ownership, API-driven automation, cross-functional security integration
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 25 percent compliance improvement, 200 hours saved annually, 30 percent reduction in user-reported issues
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: inventing specific GitLab features, assuming CI/CD tooling beyond what's documented (e.g., no 'GitLab Runner' unless stated), or fabricating GitLab-specific contributions

risk_controls: Never assume GitLab usage unless explicitly stated in source — replace with 'CI/CD pipelines' or 'automated release workflows'
Do not add metrics beyond those directly quoted in resume (e.g., no '25 percent improvement' unless tied to a specific action like phishing simulation)
Avoid generic phrases like 'trusted advisor' or 'strategic partner'; use concrete verbs: built, engineered, delivered, integrated
